LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PHUKAB MONTAKARN, PRASOMSUK LLC, AND SIAM ORCHID TRADITIONAL THAI MASSAGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO MS. PHUKAB IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON THE DEMURRER. FURTHERMORE, AS EXPLAINED IN THE COURT'S ORDER ON THE DEMURRER, PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AGAINST THE REMAINING DEFENDANTS. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kevin Sherman, Esq. 510-832-4295 of Venardi Zurada for Plaintiff.
Jessica Wahl, Esq. 415-877-4486 of Rencher Law Group for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MONTAKARN PHUKAB, PRASOMSUK LLC, SIAM ORCHID TRADITIONAL THAI MASSAGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Montakarn Phukab, Prasomsuk LLC, and Siam Orchid Traditional Thai Massage's motion to strike plaintiff Milena Levitin's second amended complaint is denied. As explained in the Court's order on the demurrer, plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for negligence against Ms. Phukab. As with demurrers, the grounds for a motion to strike must appear on the face of the complaint or from matter which is properly the subject of judicial notice. (Code Civ. Proc., 437.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kevin Sherman, Esq. 510-832-4295 of Venardi Zurada for Plaintiff.
Jessica Wahl, Esq. 415-877-4486 of Rencher Law Group for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MONTAKARN PHUKAB, PRASOMSUK LLC, SIAM ORCHID TRADITIONAL THAI MASSAGE's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Montakarn Phukab's demurrer to plaintiff Milena Levitin's second amended complaint (SAC) is overruled. On demurrer, the Court accepts as true properly pled allegations of fact in the complaint as well as facts which may be inferred from those expressly alleged. (Mez Industries, Inc. v. Pacific Nat. Ins. Co. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 856.) Defendant improperly alleges facts regarding Bhawana Thai Massage, LLC, that are not found in the SAC and as to which defendant has not properly sought judicial notice. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.70; Cal. R. Ct. 3.1306(c).)

Plaintiff was granted leave to amend in order to allege sufficient facts to support naming defendant Ms. Phukab personally liable for the alleged negligence of DOE 1. The SAC alleges the following: (1) Ms. Phukab "owned and operated Defendant Siam Orchid Traditional Thai Massage as a sole proprietorship or general partnership" (SAC 1); (2) Ms. Phukab owned and operated the business as a sole proprietorship or general partnership and is liable to plaintiff "by virtue of respondeat superior and/or agent-principle [sic] liability pursuant to Cal. Civ. Code 2338" (SAC 3); (3) Ms. Phukab is directly liable "to plaintiff for negligent hiring, retention, and supervising of Defendant DOE 1" (SAC 4); and (4) Ms. Phukab remains personally liable pursuant to Cal. Corp. Code 17710.09(c)(1)" (SAC 5-6).

Plaintiff is entitled to plead facts or legal theories in the alternative, or even inconsistently. (See Radar Co. v. Stone (1986) 178 Cal.App.3d 10, 29.) The SAC sufficiently pleads allegations that would support holding defendant Ms. Phukab personally liable as a sole proprietor. (See e.g. Twenty-Nine Palms Enterprises Corp. v. Bardos (2012) 210 Cal.App.4th 1435, 1449 "[a] sole owner is a sole proprietorship and a sole proprietorship is not a legal entity separate from its individual owner."].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs PHUKAB MONTAKARN, PRASOMSUK LLC, and SIAM ORCHID TRADITIONAL THAI MASSAGE's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint:

Defendants Montakarn Phukab, Prasomsuk LLC, Siam Orchid Traditional Thai Massage's motion to strike Plaintiff Milena Levitin's first amended complaint is denied. The motion is moot as to Ms. Phukab in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer. Furthermore, as explained in the Court's order on the demurrer, Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for negligence against the remaining defendants.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kevin Sherman, esq. (510-832-42954) of Venardi Zurada for Plaintiff.
Rachel N. Rivers, esq. (415-877-4486) of Rencher Law Group for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs PHUKAB MONTAKARN, PRASOMSUK LLC, and SIAM ORCHID TRADITIONAL THAI MASSAGE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT:

Defendants Montakarn Phukab, Prasomsuk LLC, and Siam Orchid Traditional Thai Massage's demurrer to Plaintiff Milena Levitin's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend as to Ms. Phukab and overruled as to Prasomsuk LLC and Siam Orchid. On demurrer, the Court accepts as true properly pled allegations of fact in the complaint as well as facts which may be inferred from those expressly alleged. (Mez Industries, Inc. v. Pacific Nat. Ins. Co. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 856.) Defendants improperly allege facts that are not found in the FAC and therefore those facts may not be considered. A demurrer for uncertainty is only sustained where a defendant cannot reasonably understand what claims are directed against him or her. (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Plaintiff alleges in the FAC that DOE 1 failed to render massage services with reasonable care and that as a result she sustained injuries to her shoulders, upper back, and neck which required medical treatment. Thus, she has sufficiently pled the elements of duty, breach, causation, and damages as required for a negligence cause of action. (See Wise v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1013.)

However, Ms. Phukab may not be sued in her individual capacity under these circumstances. Vicarious liability, agency liability, and negligent hiring liability theories run to the employer, Siam Orchid, rather than to an individual such as Ms. Phukab. (See Civ. Code 2338 [establishing employer liability for negligence of employees in scope of employment]; C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 877 ["Absent . . . a special relationship, there can be no individual liability to third parties for negligent hiring, retention or supervision of a fellow employee"]; Roman Catholic Bishop v. Superior Court (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1556, 1564 ["An employer may be liable to a third person for the employer's negligence in hiring or retaining an employee who is incompetent or unfit."] [emphasis added].) There are no allegations to suggest that Ms. Phukab should be individually liable for the alleged negligence of DOE 1, and Siam Orchid should adequately satisfy the policy behind negligent hiring liability. (See Phillips v. TLC Plumbing, Inc. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1133, 1139 ["Liability for negligent hiring ... is based upon the reasoning that if an enterprise hires individuals with characteristics which might pose a danger to customers or other employees, the enterprise should bear the loss caused by the wrongdoing of its incompetent or unfit employees."].) Moreover, to the extent Prasomsuk LLC is liable as a successor that assumed all past, present, and future liability of the business, it is solely liable to the exclusion of individual officers such as Ms. Phukab. (See Corp. Code 17703.04(a)(1) [liabilities of LLC are solely those of the LLC].)
20 days leave to amend.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
